candidate_problems.text: Can the conjecture be made non-tautological by specifying necessary and sufficient generative conditions for boundary formation, and by distinguishing unavoidable coordination classifications from genuinely tribal, adversarial, self-sealing group structures?

What institutional designs actually reduce reliance on identity proxies without destroying coordination efficiency—i.e., which trust, reputation, error-correction, and cross-group mechanisms change the feedback structure so that disagreement remains reversible rather than becoming hostile boundary maintenance?

Under what empirical or formal conditions does explicit anti-tribalism decrease versus intensify covert tribalization, and is there a measurable phase transition where suppressing overt markers predictably shifts status competition into morally legitimized symbolic markers such as 'epistemic standards' or 'safety'?
